_comment: Bundle-size budgets for the dual-backend (classic / EMBER_RENDER_BACKEND=gxt) rollup build. Each entry's `path` is the DIRECTORY rollup emits for that build mode: dist/prod (production, debug macros stripped) and dist/dev (debug). The gate (scripts/bundle-size-check.mjs) concatenates every *.js under the directory (sorted) and measures raw/gzip/brotli on the concatenation — the single-bundle-equivalent size of the whole backend output. Numbers below were captured from HEAD with a 5% headroom baked in; the check fails on anything more than 5% over (effective ceiling ~10.25% over HEAD). Refresh with: rm -rf dist && [EMBER_RENDER_BACKEND=gxt] npx rollup --config && node scripts/bundle-size-check.mjs <backend>. HISTORY: pre-2026-06 budgets tracked the legacy single-file AMD bundles (dist/ember.prod.js etc.) which the rollup ESM build no longer produces, so the gate was inert (every entry MISSING). Phase 2.6 (commit c8eb53d95e) externalized @lifeart/gxt + DEBUG-gated __gxtRebuildViewTreeFromDom, dropping the inlined gxt dist + its @glimmer/syntax/@handlebars/parser deps from ember.prod (~600KB raw). The 2026-06 bundle-audit follow-up repointed the gate at the real dist/prod + dist/dev trees and recaptured numbers.

classic._comment: RE-BASELINED 2026-06-13b (PROD comment-strip): the prod rollup pass (debugMacrosMode === false -> dist/prod) now drops JSDoc/line comments via babel `shouldPrintComment` (rollup.config.mjs), keeping only @__PURE__/@__NO_SIDE_EFFECTS__/@license/@preserve annotations. Comments carry no runtime behavior and every consuming app's minifier discards them anyway, so shipping them only inflated the published prod dist and this gate. Classic prod recaptured at the new (lower) floor + 5% headroom: brotli 379120 -> 207704, raw 2448998 -> 1284433. dist/dev is UNCHANGED (dev keeps comments for debuggability) so its budget is left at its prior floor. PRIOR (2026-06-13a, GXT build-time-gating): the dual-backend PR had leaked GXT integration code (gxt-bridge accessors + render machinery) into SHARED classic modules behind RUNTIME guards (getGxtRenderer()?.x) rollup could not DCE (~+19.7 kB brotli). That code is now wrapped in build-time `if (__GXT_MODE__)` blocks folded to `false` + DCE'd, and gxt-bridge.ts is marked side-effect-free so it tree-shakes out. The presence guard in bundle-size-check.mjs (assertNoGxtResidue, which strips comments before matching) fails if any LIVE gxt-bridge call site reappears, backstopping a regression a size budget alone can't catch.

gxt._comment: RE-BASELINED 2026-06-13b (PROD comment-strip): prod now strips JSDoc/line comments (see classic _comment + rollup.config.mjs `shouldPrintComment`). This is the single largest safe win for the GXT backend: the gxt-backend compat layer (manager.ts, compile.ts) is densely annotated with design-rationale prose that brotli compresses far worse than code, so >50% of several gxt chunks were comment bytes (the `manager` shared chunk dropped 585,638 -> 268,526 raw, the whole prod tree brotli 680329 -> 285252). dist/dev is UNCHANGED. WHOLE-TREE vs APP-RELEVANT: the dist is a per-module ESM tree, so an app pays only for what it imports. The whole-tree concat the gate measures here is the WORST CASE: it includes BOTH the GXT runtime template compiler (@ember/template-compilation/index.js = compile.ts, ~309KB raw stripped) AND the retained classic wire-format compiler (@ember/template-compiler/lib/template.js, ~317KB raw stripped) — NEITHER of which a precompiled app loads (verified: neither is reachable from @ember/application's import graph). A representative precompiled-app closure (@ember/application + routing + component + glimmer renderer + template-factory) is ~942KB raw / ~164KB brotli (gxt) vs ~762KB raw / ~132KB brotli (classic), i.e. the app-relevant GXT payload is +~25% brotli, NOT the +44% the whole-tree shows. The residual gxt delta is the genuine manager.ts compat layer (a from-scratch reimplementation of Ember's component-manager semantics on GXT primitives), not a tree-shaking failure. PRIOR history: @lifeart/gxt is externalized (not inlined); gxt shims @glimmer/{validator,manager,reference,destroyable}; the Glimmer VM opcode core is purged from the gxt dist (GXT_DIST_VM_STUBS + the APPEND_OPCODES marker scan in build-gxt-package, re-verified clean). Budgets below = current floor + 5% headroom.
